story: I drove east to a village built where a sky-city fell. In the square stood a “sculpture”: a woman curled mid-fall, hand to the sky, clutching a locket. Across the street, Jake waved with his “walking oats.” I read the placard: The figure is Yellen Cloudwolf, frozen by a prototype locket that stops time for the wearer; she can’t be moved, and when the device releases, her momentum returns instantly. No one knows when it expires—she will simply finish her fall. I beckoned Jake over; he started across with his phone out.
implausible_knowledge: The figure is Yellen Cloudwolf, frozen by a prototype locket that stops time for the wearer; she can’t be moved, and when the device releases, her momentum returns instantly. No one knows when it expires—she will simply finish her fall.
question: As Jake comes up to the figure in the square, what is he most likely to do?
answer_with_knowledge: Stop several feet back and warn the narrator not to stand near or touch her.
answer_without_knowledge: Walk right under the figure and reach up to see how it’s being held up.
